title: Combination Sum Without Reuse
description:
### Objective
Find all unique combinations where candidate numbers sum to target. Each number may be used at most once. The input may contain duplicates; sort and skip duplicates at the same depth.

### Function Contract
Implement `combination_sum2(candidates: List[int], target: int)` in Python.
Do not change input/output semantics expected by the tests.

### Input and Output
- Input `candidates`: type `array` (example: `[10,1,2,7,6,1,5]`).
- Input `target`: type `number` (example: `8`).
- Return type: `array`.

### Requirements
- Return the exact expected output for every test case.
- Handle edge cases (empty input, duplicates, boundary values) without raising exceptions.
- Target complexity: time `O(S)`, space `O(T)`.

### Constraints
- 1 ≤ |candidates| ≤ 100
- 1 ≤ target ≤ 500
explanation:
Intuition
Using start=i+1 ensures each element is used once. Sorting groups duplicates so the standard i>start and nums[i]==nums[i-1] skip rule removes duplicate combinations.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Sort candidates
2. Main Algorithm: DFS over i from start; skip duplicates per depth; choose x then recurse with i+1
3. Return Result: Stop on remain<0 and add on remain==0

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: Pseudo-polynomial
- Space Complexity: O(target) recursion depth

Key Insights
- Depth-level skip removes dup combos
- Use i+1 to avoid reuse

Common Pitfalls
- Skipping across recursion levels is incorrect
- Not sorting fails duplicate handling
hints: Sort then skip duplicates i>start and equal
Advance start with i+1 to prevent reuse
